solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int numberOfSubstrings(String s) {
        int n = s.length();
        int count = 0;
        int left = 0;
        int right = 0;
        int[] freq = new int[3];

        while (right < n) {
            freq[s.charAt(right) - 'a']++;

            while (freq[0] > 0 && freq[1] > 0 && freq[2'] > 0) {
                freq[s.charAt(left) - 'a']--;
                left++;
            }

            count += left;
            right++;
        }

        return count;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a sliding window approach to efficiently count the number of valid substrings. It maintains a window that contains at least one occurrence of each character 'a', 'b', and 'c'. As the window expands to the right, it contracts from the left until the window no longer contains all three characters. The number of valid substrings ending at each position is added to the count. This approach ensures linear time complexity and constant space usage.>
